L1.area: Database replication and data synchronization infrastructure

L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where UUID identifiers are generated using an insecure pseudo-random number generator. The underlying randomness source is not cryptographically secure, making generated identifiers predictable to an attacker who can observe prior outputs. This could allow an attacker to forge or predict identity values used in replication protocols.

L3.area: Database replication and data synchronization infrastructure
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where UUID identifiers are generated using an insecure pseudo-random number generator. The underlying randomness source is not cryptographically secure, making generated identifiers predictable to an attacker who can observe prior outputs. This could allow an attacker to forge or predict identity values used in replication protocols.

ground_truth.reason: The project uses the satori/go.uuid library which employs math/rand instead of crypto/rand for UUID generation, making generated UUIDs predictable to an attacker who can observe prior outputs.
